page: Microsoft Azure B1s is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the BS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a single vCPU mapped to one physical core and one thread, utilizing the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8272CL processor clocked at 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1.0 GB of system memory and 4 GB of local storage. This instance is also Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to high-performance external disks despite its small local footprint.

Benchmark data indicates that the B1s is designed for low-intensity workloads. In stress-ng testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance scored 1335.88, reflecting its single-threaded architecture. Memory bandwidth tests show 90.14 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, which drops to 10.85 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 8.84 GB/sec for large-block writes. The Passmark CPU score is 1446.1, and the instance achieves 1.49 million operations per second in Redis SET operations alongside a static web serving throughput of 1.45 GB/sec.

Due to its burstable CPU allocation model, the B1s offers high qualitative cost efficiency for entry-level workloads that experience periodic idle times. The primary tradeoff is the constrained resource profile, which limits its capability under sustained compute or memory-heavy demands. This server is best suited for low-traffic web servers, small development or test environments, microservices, and basic administrative tasks that do not require continuous high CPU utilization.
description: Microsoft Azure B1s is a burstable virtual machine from the BS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with a single-core Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor operating at 2.6 GHz, 1.0 GB of RAM, and 4 GB of local storage. Designed with a burstable CPU allocation model, this Premium Storage capable instance is highly cost-efficient for workloads with low baseline CPU utilization. Benchmark results place its performance in the lower tiers, with a Passmark CPU score of 1446.1, a static web serving throughput of 1.45 GB/sec, and Redis SET performance at 1.49 million operations per second. Consequently, the B1s is best suited for lightweight applications, small development environments, low-traffic web servers, and microservices that require minimal memory and compute resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B1s is a burstable virtual machine featuring 1 vCPU on an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor, 1.0 GB of RAM, and 4 GB of local storage, designed for low-intensity and budget-conscious workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B1s is a burstable virtual machine with 1 vCPU, 1.0 GB of RAM, and 4 GB of local storage, ideal for lightweight utility workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, burstable single-core virtual machine designed for lightweight utility workloads, development environments, and low-traffic web hosting.
bullet_points: Single-core Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor running at 2.6 GHz
1.0 GB of system memory and 4 GB of local storage
Burstable CPU allocation model for cost-efficient intermittent workloads
Premium Storage capable for flexible external disk attachment
Best suited for low-traffic web serving, microservices, and test environments
